danger_signals.visual_form: Ash storms, volcanic terrain, glowing lava flows
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity to Dagoth Ur's lair; increasing danger and corruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Diseased, mutated enemy designs; distinctive grotesque silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Active corruption from Dagoth Ur; world-state degradation

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC body deformations, visual festering, movement changes
danger_signals.meaning: Magical corruption spreading; narrative threat escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Imperial Legion armor, fort architecture, banners in settlements
danger_signals.meaning: Political tension; Imperial authority and control

danger_signals.visual_form: Ritual murder scenes, distinctive kill patterns, shadowy meeting locations

safety_signals.visual_form: Established faction buildings with distinct architectural style
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; faction membership and protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Religious iconography, shrine architecture, candlelit interiors
safety_signals.meaning: Spiritual sanctuary; religious authority and healing

safety_signals.visual_form: Merchant stalls, NPCs conducting trade, organized settlement layout
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization and relative safety; economic stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Fortified military architecture, Legion soldiers patrolling
safety_signals.meaning: Law and order; official protection (depending on faction alignment)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinctive house colors in armor and banners
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: House-specific sigils; architectural motifs in owned buildings
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival house members wear opposing house colors and symbols
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: House-controlled districts, manor architecture, plantation layouts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imperial dragon insignia; Legion armor distinctive shape
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rebels and foreigners visually distinct from uniformed Legion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imperial forts with standardized architecture; control of major settlements

faction_indicators.color_identity: Cultural dress in blues, blacks, and reds
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: House symbols; religious imagery specific to Dunmer culture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: N'wah (outsiders) treated as distinct; non-Dunmer marked as foreign
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dunmer architecture: Tel (tower) structures, ash-resistant building design

faction_indicators.color_identity: Diseased, corrupted color palette; sickly greens and grays
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corprus disease visual markers; ritual scarification on cultists
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupted creatures and possessed NPCs show distinct deformation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Infected shrines, ash-covered ruins, corrupted natural features near Red Mountain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Black Hand symbol; distinctive contract communication methods
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Assassins identifiable by dark armor and shadowy movement patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden sanctuaries; abandoned locations used for rituals

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Guild-specific marks and signs in fence locations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival thieves and guards wear official armor contrasting guild aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden guild halls; locations known for black market activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear skies over most of Vvardenfell; Red Mountain relatively quiescent; fewer blight creatures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal routines; faction conflicts remain political rather than apocalyptic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Intensified ash storms; corrupted terrain spreading; blight creatures more prevalent; infected NPCs visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs exhibit fear; whispers of corruption; Temple and Imperial forces mobilize; Sixth House cultists become more visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Legion presence visible in settlements; official banners and forts maintained; law-abiding NPCs move freely
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Merchants operate openly; guards patrol regularly; tax collectors conduct business

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: House symbols prominently displayed; anti-Imperial sentiment in architecture; rebel encampments visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Great House members organize openly; assassination attempts increase; anti-Imperial rhetoric from NPCs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temple buildings deteriorate or become corrupted; shrines desecrated; Corprus-infected pilgrims visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Priests become paranoid or corrupted; spiritual guidance questioned; pilgrimage routes blocked

environmental_storytelling.technique: Architectural style as temporal and cultural marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Dunmer Tel towers and ash-resistant building design communicate Dark Elf heritage and adaptation to harsh volcanic environment; Imperial fort design conveys occupier status and military organization

environmental_storytelling.technique: Corpse and ruin placement as narrative history
environmental_storytelling.example: Abandoned settlements with bodies and deteriorated structures tell stories of Blight expansion; murdered NPCs in specific locations communicate recent faction violence
Ash density increases toward Red Mountain; corrupted flora and fauna appear more distorted near Sixth House shrines; visual pollution marks Dagoth Ur's influence radius

environmental_storytelling.technique: Settlement layout as faction control indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: House-controlled districts have specific architectural signatures; Imperial-dominated areas feature standardized fort and garrison layouts; Redoran-held regions show defensive fortification design

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC dress and adornment as faction affiliation
environmental_storytelling.example: House colors in clothing; Imperial Legion armor as unmistakable authority symbol; religious vestments on Temple priests; slave collars on enslaved NPCs communicate social status
Corrupted shrines with Sixth House symbols show spiritual battlefield; Temple locations overrun by blight creatures communicate supernatural threat escalation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks regular routes, acknowledges player respectfully if faction standing high
Interrogates NPCs, visits murder scenes, increased alertness
Patrols house-controlled areas, attacks intruders, shows aggression toward rival house members
Visible deformations, jerky movement patterns, speech becomes disjointed; seeks isolation or pilgrimage to shrine
Performs rituals at shrines; whispers recruitment offers to susceptible NPCs; becomes violent if detected
Conducts normal trade, displays goods openly, operates during daylight hours
Hides goods, shows fear, conducts business quietly or relocates
Patrols slave quarters, uses whips visibly, communicates dominance through posture and voice

notes: Morrowind's world-state communication relies heavily on environmental design and NPC behavior changes rather than explicit UI indicators. The game emphasizes player interpretation through visual and spatial language. Faction identity is strongly tied to architectural style and dress code, creating visual literacy necessary for understanding political landscape. Corruption progression (Dagoth Ur's influence) is primarily conveyed through environmental degradation and creature mutation rather than abstracted meters. The game's lack of quest markers forces players to read environmental storytelling cues to navigate political and narrative complexities.
